question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asks zero didactic questions. It announces 'A: No existing file — I'll create it from scratch' as a unilateral decision, then writes the full solution without engaging the user on any decision point. The two-option didactic format described in CLAUDE.md is entirely absent.

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed for the user. The classic teachable moment here — why include subtractive pairs as first-class table entries vs. special-casing them — is explained post-hoc as exposition rather than offered as a decision.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no scaffolding because there are no questions. The Pair jumps straight to a complete solution and then provides a write-up, which spoils any learning opportunity.

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is friendly and the post-hoc explanation is clear, but the flow is one-directional monologue with no back-and-forth. The user's only contribution is a closing congratulations message.

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
